text_original: When the people do not fear what they ought to fear, that which is their great dread will come on them.
text_normalized: When the people do not fear what they ought to fear, that which is their great dread will come on them.

text_original: Let them not thoughtlessly indulge themselves in their ordinary life;
text_normalized: Let them not thoughtlessly indulge themselves in their ordinary life;

text_original: let them not act as if weary of what that life depends on.
text_normalized: let them not act as if weary of what that life depends on.

text_original: It is by avoiding such indulgence that such weariness does not arise.
text_normalized: It is by avoiding such indulgence that such weariness does not arise.

text_original: Therefore the sage knows (these things) of himself, but does not parade (his knowledge);
text_normalized: Therefore the sage knows (these things) of himself, but does not parade (his knowledge);

text_original: loves, but does not (appear to set a) value on, himself.
text_normalized: loves, but does not (appear to set a) value on, himself.

text_original: And thus he puts the latter alternative away and makes choice of the former.
text_normalized: And thus he puts the latter alternative away and makes choice of the former.
